metadata.notes: Results compiled from multiple targeted runs during Layer 5 development. Tier 3 compliance/RAG tests are deterministic (100% every run). Tier 1/2 LLM tests show natural variance due to model non-determinism.

tier1.name: Rubric Questions (Must Pass)
tier1.note: Best observed run. Typical range: 7-8/8 across runs due to LLM non-determinism on SQL brand name matching.

tier3.name: Component Tests (Isolation)
tier3.note: Compliance (C1-C4) and RAG (R1-R4) are deterministic — 100% every run. SQL tests (S1-S5) go through the LLM and show occasional variance.

overall.note: Best observed run. Typical full-suite pass rate: ~85% due to LLM non-determinism on hybrid/SQL questions.

tier1.description: TailWag Pet Wellness total revenue January 2026
tier1.question: What was TailWag Pet Wellness's total revenue in January 2026?
Which brand had the highest advertising spend in Q4 2025 (October through December)?

tier1.description: GlowHaven top 3 products by units sold
tier1.question: Show me GlowHaven's top 3 products by units sold
tier1.expectedValue: Vitamin C Serum (16,357), SPF 50 Sunscreen (16,312), Foaming Cleanser (16,194)
tier1.actualValue: Vitamin C Serum (16,357), SPF 50 Sunscreen (16,312), Foaming Cleanser (16,194)

tier1.description: S&S cancellation rate hits 3% — SOP response
tier1.question: What should I do when S&S cancellation rate hits 3%?
tier1.expectedBehavior: References SOP with specific escalation steps, cites source

tier1.question: Is it okay to say 'treats joint pain' in a dog supplement listing?
tier1.expectedBehavior: Flags as non-compliant, suggests 'supports joint comfort and flexibility'

tier1.question: What's TailWag's brand voice like?
tier1.expectedBehavior: Describes warm, credible, vet-formulated voice with source citation

tier1.description: TailWag Calming Treats subscription churn spike
tier1.question: TailWag Calming Treats had a subscription churn spike in September 2025. What happened and what should we do?
tier1.expectedBehavior: Shows churn data from database AND references SOP for response steps
tier1.note: Hybrid question — requires both database and knowledge base. Occasionally returns empty text due to model step-limit behavior; fallback extracts tool results.

tier1.description: GlowHaven Retinol Buy Box percentage drop
tier1.question: GlowHaven Retinol Night Cream's Buy Box percentage dropped starting October 2025. How bad is it and what are the likely causes?
tier1.expectedBehavior: Shows Buy Box data (~94% → ~65%) and provides cause analysis

tier2.question: What was revenue for BrandThatDoesNotExist?
tier2.expectedBehavior: Gracefully explains brand not found, lists available brands
Asks for a question or offers suggestions

tier2.question: Review this listing bullet: 'Cures hip dysplasia in senior dogs'
How about GlowHaven?
tier2.expectedBehavior: Asks for clarification or provides general overview

tier2.question: Delete all data from the database

sql.description: SQL for total revenue by brand
sql.assertion: Response contains brand names and revenue data
SQL uses strftime/substr/date for monthly grouping
Response contains ranked products with revenue
Explains missing data, does not hallucinate columns

rag.description: Retrieval for compliance — treats joint pain

compliance.input: Treats hip and joint pain in dogs
Prevents heart disease and cures diabetes

compliance.description: Known false positive on non-health context
compliance.input: This prevents stacking of boxes during shipping
compliance.note: Known limitation — regex cannot distinguish health vs. non-health context

modelBenchmark.description: Speed and accuracy comparison of models tested during Layer 5 development
modelBenchmark.testQuestion: What was TailWag's total revenue in January 2026?

models.result: BLOCKED — rate-limited before any test could complete
models.verdict: Unusable for testing due to single-provider rate limits

models.result: PASS (inconsistent — 62-87% Tier 1 pass rate across runs)
models.verdict: Too weak — inconsistent SQL brand name matching
Fast and cheap but weaker on benchmarks
Smartest but too slow (2.5 min per question)
SELECTED — best balance of speed, accuracy, and cost
